title: Pseudotrimezia cathartica
taxonomy: Pseudotrimezia cathartica is classified within the family Iridaceae, which consists of a diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as irises. It is further classified within the subfamily Iridoideae and tribe Irideae. The genus Pseudotrimezia is a group of flowering plants endemic to Brazil, and Pseudotrimezia cathartica is one of the species within this genus. The classification of Pseudotrimezia cathartica reflects its evolutionary relationships with other irises and its unique characteristics as a member of the Iridaceae family. Pseudotrimezia cathartica is a plant species that has been studied and described by botanists, providing valuable insights into its classification and evolutionary history.

intro: Pseudotrimezia cathartica is a species of plant in the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the "Cathartic Flower" due to its purgative properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its ability to treat digestive issues such as constipation and indigestion, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pseudotrimezia cathartica include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
